notes.subject: Physics
notes.title: Electricity
notes.content:
[{"insert":"Electricity notes

Current :: the rate of flow of charge
Potential difference :: the work done per unit of positive
EMF :: the total energy supplied per coulomb of charge by the cell.
Resistance :: a materials opposition to the flow of electric charge
Ohm’s Law :: the current through a conductor is directly proportional to the potential difference across the conductor at a constant temperature
Diodes :: electric components that allows current to flow in one direction in a circuit.
Conventional current :: when positive charge flows from the positive terminal of the battery through the circuit and back to the negative terminal of the battery
What are the factors that affect resistance? :: the type of metal used, thickness, length  and the temperature

In series:
* Current is the same everywhere
* Voltage is divided between each resistor in ratio
* Adding a resistor in series increases the resistance thus decreasing total current
* Adding a cell increases the total potential difference and total current

In parallel: 
* Voltage is the same 
* Current gets divided into the parallel branches
* Adding a resistor will decrease total resistance thus increasing total current
* Adding a cell will just increase how long the circuit lasts

Diodes:
* Includes LEDs( light emitting diodes) 
* They have a cathode and a anode
* In forward bias: the charge passes through the anode and then through the cathode of the diode allowing current to flow through the circuit
* In reverse bias: the charge passes through the cathode and then the anode, which is in reverse thus preventing the flow of charge.
* It has low resistance(when connected correctly ) and Is considered as non-ohmic
* When connected right, they are hardy(tuff) and highly effiecent. They turn on and off much faster than filament. Also low resistance
* Can be permanently damaged when connected in reverse bias to a high potential difference 
* The only work at(fancy words: only conduct at ) a particular potential difference known as the knee potential difference or knee voltage
* They have a maximum current rating(they have low resistance, so can’t handle to much current), thus they have a resistor in series to protect the diode.

"}]

notes.subject: English
notes.title: types of sentences
notes.content:
[{"insert":"Simple sentences:"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"header":3}},{"insert":"• a sentence with a finite verb
• it has no conjunction
• Example: Ketania walks to the shops. Keshayen swims at Alcatraz swim club
simple sentence definition :: a sentence with a finite verb 
does a simple sentence contain a conjuction :: non
Compound sentences:"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"header":3}},{"insert":"• Two main clauses (simple sentences) joined with a conjunction
• uses coordinating conjunctions(FANBOYS)
• Examples: She walks to the shops and she buys food.
compound sentence definition :: a sentence made up by two main clauses joined by a conjunction. 
does a compound sentence contain a conjuction and if so what type:: it contains coordination conjuctions(FANBOYS)
Complex sentences:"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"header":3}},{"insert":"• One main clause joined by subordinating clause
• uses subordinationg conjunctions (a white bus) but just remember everything besides fanboys is subordingating
• Examples: she walks to the shops because her vehicle broken. because her car is broken, she walks to the shops.
• there are two types, loose complex sentences and periodic complex sentences. periodic complex sentences have the main clause last and loose have the main clause first in the sentence
complex sentence definition:: a sentence made up by a main clause and a subordinating 
clause does a complex sentence have a conjunction and if so what type :: it contains a subordinating conjunction 
what are the two types of complex sentences:: periodic and loose complex sentences
"}]

notes.subject: Geography
notes.title: The atmosphere
notes.content:
[{"insert":"Structure of the atmosphere:"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"header":2}},{"insert":"Definition: layer of gases that surround or envelop the earth."},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"blockquote":true}},{"insert":"extra terminology:"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"header":3}},{"insert":"positive lapse rate: a decrease in temperature with an increase of altitude."},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"negative lapse rate: a increase in temperature with an increase of altitude"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"
The troposphere:"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"header":3}},{"insert":"the layer closest to earth's surface(0km -> ±12km)"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"All weather occurs in this layer"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"formation of clouds occur here"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"all gases necessary for life are contained here"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"temperature decreases with altitude from 14*c to -50*c(Positive lapse rate)"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"Tropopause: ","attributes":{"bold":true}},{"insert":"the upper level of the troposphere where temperature remains constant with increasing altitude"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"
The statosphere:"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"header":3}},{"insert":"It contains the ozone layer(O3)"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"there is a temperature inversion, temps get warmer from -50*c to -10*c with increasing altitude due to the ozone layer absorbing UV radiation."},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"Stratopause: the upper level of the stratosphere where temperature remains constant with increaseing altitude."},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"
The mesosphere:"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"header":3}},{"insert":"Temperature decreases with increasing altitude  from -10*c to about -80*c."},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"this layer protects us from meteorides due to it being the first layer with an increase in gas denisity(not enough to breath though)"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"mesopause: upper level of the mesosphere where temperature remains constant with increaseing altitude."},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"
The thermosphere:"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"header":3}},{"insert":"consists of the exosphere and the ionsphere."},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"Another temperature inversion is cause due to nitrogen and oxygen absorbing UV energy. "},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"temperatures can range from -80*c to about 1200+*c."},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"
Composition of the atmosphere:"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"header":3}},{"insert":"made up of gases, liquids and solids."},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"there are two types of gases in the atmosphere, Permanent and variable gases."},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"permanent gases - % does not change. these are nitrogen(78), oxygen(21) and argon(1). **these are rounded**"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"variables gases - the % of these change depending on factors and include water vapour, carbon dioxide and methane."},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"liqueds in the atmosphere is water droplets(they are microscopic)"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"solids include dust, pollen, salt, hail and snow. these form "},{"insert":"condensation nuclei ","attributes":{"bold":true}},{"insert":"- small surfaces onto which water vapour can condense to form cloud droplets"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"

Importance:"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"header":3}},{"insert":"Has the gases which are responsible for photosynthesis(CO2)."},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"Oxygen for respiration"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"Ozone protects us against UV radiation"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"has water vapour for all our weather."},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"Greanhouse gases(CO2) absorb radiation to keep the atmostphere warm (this is natural GHE). enhanced GHE is caused by man and is global warming "},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"meteorite protection"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"
Ozone in the atmosphere:"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"header":3}},{"insert":"ozone is made of 3 oxygen atoms"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"mostly found in the stratosphere(good) and some in the troposphere(bad)"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"it absorbs UV radiation"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"normal levels of ozone is 300 Dobson units(Du) but when it goes below 220Du a whole is said to be formed"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"
"},{"insert":"ozone layer depletion cause:","attributes":{"bold":true}},{"insert":"
Human activity(industry)"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"the release of chlorofluorocarbons(CFC's) from cooling agents such as fridges and aerosol cans."},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"high altitude flights"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"
"},{"insert":"Process of ozone depletion: ","attributes":{"bold":true}},{"insert":"
UV radiation hits the CFC molecule"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"ordered"}},{"insert":"chlorine atom breaks away"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"ordered"}},{"insert":"the chlorine atom hits the ozone molecule and takes one oxygen atom to make chlorine monoxide and leaves and oxygen molecule(O2)"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"ordered"}},{"insert":"an oxygen atom hits the chlorine monoxide molecule cause the chlorine atom to break away and make 02 and leave the chlorine to continue the cycle again."},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"ordered"}},{"insert":"
"},{"insert":"Effects from ozone depletion:","attributes":{"bold":true}},{"insert":"
weakens peoples immune systems'"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"causes eye cataracts"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"skin cancer"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"decreases phytoplankton in oceans"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"disrupts photosynthesis killing crop yields"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"increases surface temperatures"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"severe weather"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"
Methods to reduce ozone depletion:
CFC free products"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"Montreal Protocol of 1987: countries agreed to phase out the production of CFCs by 1996"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"less high altitude flights"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"plant trees for more O2"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"check aircons and fridges for leaks"},{"insert":"
","attributes":{"list":"bullet"}},{"insert":"

"}]
